rag_rules: Place input assumptions in component type annexes when bounds are concrete or visible
Use assign only for implementation-level bindings of existing outputs or auxiliary variables
Avoid placing guarantees in implementation scope; use type scope for input assumptions and output guarantees
Ensure AGREE types match the actual types of expressions (bool for comparisons, int for integer operations, real for real operations)
generic_rules: Use visible feature names in AGREE contracts when they are in scope
Avoid direct use of subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `eq` for equation declarations and `assign` for implementation-level bindings
Use `guarantee` for output range guarantees in component type scope
Use `assume` for input envelope assumptions in component type scope
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantees in implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names are not AGREE variables
